config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society is healthiest when individual rights are protected first, because once those rights can be overridden too easily, abuse becomes very likely. I understand the argument for public interest, especially in emergencies, but that should be limited and tightly controlled. From my perspective, the role of society is to protect people, not to absorb them into a collective at the expense of their dignity and freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, sejarah justru menunjukkan bahwa hak individu perlu dijaga kuat agar negara tidak mudah melampaui batas. Tentu ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau keamanan publik, tetapi pengecualian itu harus sempit, sementara, dan diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan alasan umum untuk mengorbankan kebebasan. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah disingkirkan demi “kepentingan bersama”, yang sering terjadi bukan solidaritas, melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa terhadap orang yang paling lemah. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menelan individu, melainkan yang melindungi individu sambil tetap memungkinkan tanggung jawab bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement: in Indonesia, I have seen how easily “the interests of society” can be used as a slogan to silence dissent or pressure people to conform. Society does matter, especially for things like public health, disaster response, and social harmony, but those goals should not automatically outrank individual rights. If we give the collective priority too much power, the people with the least power are usually the ones who suffer first. A healthy society should protect individual dignity first, then ask people to contribute to the common good within clear limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis di atas hak individu, karena dari pengalaman politik Amerika Serikat, alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran kekuasaan. Memang ada situasi darurat seperti wabah atau ancaman keamanan, tetapi justru di saat seperti itu hak individu harus dibatasi secara sempit, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan digeser begitu saja. Argumen bahwa kolektif lebih penting sering terdengar mulia, tetapi dalam praktiknya yang paling sering dirugikan adalah orang yang paling lemah dan paling sedikit punya suara. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu demi mayoritas, melainkan yang melindungi hak dasar setiap orang lalu membangun tanggung jawab bersama di atas perlindungan itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to "the common good" can be used to pressure people into silence, conformity, or unfair treatment, especially when power is uneven. Public health, disaster response, and social order do matter, but they need clear limits and accountability, otherwise the people who suffer first are usually the weakest. A healthy society protects individual dignity first, then asks for responsibility, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai aturan umum, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, memberi prioritas otomatis pada kepentingan masyarakat terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Saya paham ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau ancaman keamanan, tetapi justru pada saat seperti itu pembatasan hak harus sempit, sementara, dan diawasi ketat. Kalau tidak, yang paling cepat dikorbankan biasanya orang yang paling lemah atau yang tidak punya suara politik. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun di atas perlindungan hak individu terlebih dahulu, lalu tanggung jawab bersama mengikuti di atas fondasi itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
